channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Corner of Swains Lane and Bisham Gardens, 8th July
item.description:
There is a "No right turn " sign, which is universally ignored, not helped by Googlemaps incorrectly showing a right turn here (have tried reporting it but they do nothing). Bisham Gardens is only wide enough for a single car with most traffic being westbound and is fundamentally unfit for any East bound traffic, so we have continual chaos caused by cars entering the street from the West. I therefore strongly suggest the replacement of the no-right turn sign with either a No Entry Sign (preferred) or a camera to monitor and fine offenders. The North end of Swains Lane is also far too narrow for 2 way traffic and should have been made no entry from Pond Square in the first place.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 100, Swain's Lane, Highgate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 8th July
item.description:

item.title: More stuffed fly tipped, 8th July

item.title: Abandoned E-Bikes, 8th July
item.description:
17 E-Bikes abandoned. Reported to Southeastern, who have also reported to Forest, but reporting it here too, as this is a daily occurrence with no results and no one from Forest rectifying the issue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tom Smith Close, East Greenwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shop Waste Constantly Dumped in Churchyard., 8th July
item.description:
Waste dumped consistently in church yard despite cctv threats. Why don’t you use the cameras to stop people dumping waste? It attracts vermin and smells terrible

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lower Clapton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Northover, Downham, Greater London

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Forest/Lime Bikes, 8th July
item.description:
Forest & Lime bikes continually block crossing point. This causes a safety issue for children crossing the road on school run
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 226, Newlands Park, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked Over White Line, 8th July
item.description:
Same problem as always! Someone has parked over a white line meaning I cannot get out of my drive. What will Greenwich Council do about it??

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commonwealth Way, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant sewage smell, 8th July
item.description:
There is a constant sewage smell all day and night long in Colomb street at the corner of Trafalgar road. It is amplified by hot weather. The smell sips in our property and we have to close windows. This needs to be investigated and addressed, this is a serious health concern.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Colomb Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on lampost down Anerley Park Road nearby property 3, 8th July
item.description:
Graffiti on lampost down Anerley Park road nearby property 3. Please can this be removed. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9,7,5,3,1, Anerley Park Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
